Annual Exam - 2023

Code: 65/5/3 (set-3)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  3. Please check that this question paper contains 38 questions.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidates will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Question paper is divided into FIVE Sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A - Question Number 1 to 18 are Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ) type and Question Number 19 & 20 are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B - Question Number 21 to 25 are Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C - Question Number 26 to 31 are Short Answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D - Question Number 32 to 35 are Long Answer (LA) type questions carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E - Question Number 36 to 38 are case study based questions carrying 4 marks each where 2 VSA type questions are of 1 mark each and 1 SA type question is of 2 marks. Internal choice is provided in 2 marks question in each case-study.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section - B, 3 questions in Section - C, 2 questions in Section - D and 2 questions in Section - E.
  9. Use of calculators is NOT allowed.

SECTION - A
(Multiple Choice Questions)
Each question carries 1 mark.

Select the correct option out of the four given options:
1.

Let R be the relation in the set N given by R = {(a, b) : a = b – 2, b > 6}.

a. (2, 4) {tex}\in{/tex} R
b. (3, 8) {tex}\in{/tex} R
c. (6, 8) {tex}\in{/tex} R
d. (8, 7) {tex}\in{/tex} R
2.

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & \text x \\ \text y & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and A = AT, where AT is the transpose of the matrix A, then

a. x = 0, y = 5
b. x + y = 5
c. x = y
d. x = 5, y = 0
3.

sin{tex}\left[\frac{\pi}{3}+\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)\right]{/tex} is equal to

a. 1
b. {tex}\frac 12{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac 13{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac 14{/tex}
4.

If for a square matrix A, A2 - A + I = O, then A-1 equals

a. A
b. A + I
c. I - A
d. A - I
5.

If {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} \alpha & 3 & 4 \\ 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 4 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0, then the value of {tex}\alpha{/tex} is

a. 1
b. 2
c. 3
d. 4
6.

If f(x) = |cos x|, then {tex}\text f\left(\frac{3 \pi}{4}\right){/tex} is

a. 1
b. -1
c. {tex}\frac{-1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
7.

If x = A cos 4t + B sin 4t, then {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \text x}{\mathrm{dt}^2}{/tex} is equal to

a. x
b. -x
c. 16x
d. -16x
8.

The function f(x) = [x], where [x] denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to x, is continuous at

a. x = 1
b. x = 1.5
c. x = -2
d. x = 4
9.

The function f(x) = x3 + 3x is increasing in interval

a. {tex}(-\infty, 0){/tex}
b. {tex}(0, \infty){/tex}
c. {tex}\mathbb{R}{/tex}
d. (0, 1)
10.

{tex}\int\limits_{-1}^1 \frac{|\text x-2|}{\text x-2}{/tex}dx, x {tex}\neq{/tex} 2 is equal to

a. 1
b. -1
c. 2
d. -2
11.

{tex}\int \frac{\sec \text x}{\sec \text x-\tan \text x}{/tex}dx equals

a. sec x - tan x + c
b. sec x + tan x + c
c. tan x - sec x + c
d. -(sec x + tan x) + c
12.

The order and the degree of the differential equation {tex}\left(1+3 \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}\right)^2{/tex} = {tex}4 \frac{\mathrm{d}^3 y}{\mathrm{~d} x^3}{/tex} respectively are:

a. {tex}1, \frac{2}{3}{/tex}
b. 3, 1
c. 3, 3
d. 1, 2
13.

If {tex}\vec{\text a} \cdot \hat{\text i}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{\text a} \cdot(\hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}){/tex} = {tex}\vec{\text a} \cdot(\hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}+\hat{\text k}){/tex} = 1, then {tex}\vec{\text a}{/tex} is

a. {tex}\hat {\text k}{/tex}
b. {tex}\hat {\text i}{/tex}
c. {tex}\hat {\text j}{/tex}
d. {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}
14.

Five fair coins are tossed simultaneously. The probability of the events that atleast one head comes up is

a. {tex}\frac{27}{32}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{5}{32}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{31}{32}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{1}{32}{/tex}
15.

If for any two events A and B, P(A) = {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex} and P(A {tex}\cap{/tex} B) = {tex}\frac{7}{10}{/tex}, then {tex}\mathrm{P}\left(\frac {\mathrm{B}}{\mathrm{A}}\right){/tex} is equal to

a. {tex}\frac {1}{10}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac {1}{8}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac {7}{8}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac {17}{20}{/tex}
16.

The angle between the lines 2x = 3y = -z and 6x = -y = -4z is

a. 0o
b. 30o
c. 45o
d. 90o
17.

If a line makes angles 90o, 135o and 45o with the x, y and z axes respectively, then its direction cosines are

a. {tex}0,\;-\frac{{1}}{{\sqrt 2 }},\;\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{/tex}
b. {tex}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}, 0, \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}, 0, -\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
d. {tex}0,\;\frac{{1}}{{\sqrt 2 }},\;\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{/tex}
18.

The magnitude of the vector {tex}6 \hat{\text i}-2 \hat{\text j}+3 \hat{\text k}{/tex} is

a. 1
b. 5
c. 7
d. 12

Assertion - Reason Based Questions

In the following questions 19 and 20, a statement of Assertion (A) is followed by a statement of Reason (R). Choose the correct answer out of the following choices:
19.

Assertion (A): {tex}\int\limits_2^8 \frac{\sqrt{10-\text x}}{\sqrt{\text x}+\sqrt{10-\text x}} \mathrm{~dx}{/tex} = 3
Reason (R): {tex}\int\limits_\text a^\text b {/tex}f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_\text a^\text b {/tex}f(a + b - x) dx

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
20.

Assertion (A): Two coins are tossed simultaneously. The probability of getting two heads, if it is known that at least one head comes up, is {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}.
Reason (R): Let E and F be two events with a random experiment, then {tex}\mathrm{P}\left(\frac {\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{E}}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{E} \cap \mathrm{F})}{\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{E})}{/tex}.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.

SECTION - B

This section comprises of Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each.
21.(i)

Find the value of k for which the function f given as
f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{cl} \frac{1-\cos x}{2 x^2}, & \text { if } x \neq 0 \\ \mathrm{k} \;\;\;\;\;\;\;, & \text { if } x=0 \end{array}\right.{/tex} is continuous at x = 0.

OR
(ii)

If x = a cos t and y = b sin t, then find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{\text d}^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}{/tex}.
22.

Find the value of tan-1{tex}\left[2 \cos \left(2 \sin ^{-1} \frac{1}{2}\right)\right]{/tex} + tan-1 1.
23.

Find the vector and the cartesian equations of a line that passes through the point A(1, 2, -1) and parallel to the line 5x - 25 = 14 - 7y = 35z.
24.

Sketch the region bounded by the lines 2x + y = 8, y = 2, y = 4 and the y-axis. Hence, obtain its area using integration.
25.(i)

If vectors {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} are such that {tex} | \vec { a } | = 3,{/tex} {tex} | \vec { b } | = \frac 2 3{/tex} and {tex} \vec { a } \times \vec { b }{/tex} is a unit vector, then write the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }.{/tex}

OR
(ii)

Find the area of the parallelogram whose adjacent sides are determined by the vectors {tex}\vec a = \hat i - \hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 2\hat i - 7\hat j + \hat k{/tex}

SECTION - C

This section comprises of Short Answer (SA) type questions of 3 marks each.
26.

Prove that the determinant {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex} is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}.
27.

Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by y = mx (m > 0), x = 1, x = 2 and the x-axis.
28.(i)

Find the coordinates of the foot of the perpendicular drawn from point (5, 7, 3) to the line {tex}\frac{\text x-15}{3}=\frac{\text y-29}{8}=\frac{\mathrm{z}-5}{-5}{/tex}.

OR
(ii)

If {tex}\vec { a } = \hat { i } + \hat { j } + \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex} then find a unit vector perpendicular to both {tex}\vec { a } + \vec { b }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { a } - \vec { b }{/tex}.
29.

Find the distance between the lines:
{tex}\vec{r}{/tex} = {tex}(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} + {tex}\lambda(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex};
{tex}\vec{r}{/tex} = {tex}(3 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}){/tex} + {tex}\mu(4 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}+12 \hat{k}){/tex}
30.(i)

Differentiate sec-1{tex}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\right){/tex} w.r.t. sin-1{tex}\left(2 x \sqrt{1-x^2}\right){/tex}.

OR
(ii)

If y = tan x + sec x, prove that {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)^{2}}{/tex}
31.(i)

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{e}^{\sin \text x}} \mathrm{~dx}{/tex}

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\text x^4}{(\text x-1)\left(\text x^2+1\right)} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}

SECTION-D

This section comprises of Long Answer (LA) type questions of 5 marks each.
32.

Solve the following Linear Programming Problem graphically:
Minimise: Z = 60x + 80y
subject to constraints:
3x + 4y {tex}\geq{/tex} 8
5x + 2y {tex}\geq{/tex} 11
x, y {tex}\geq{/tex} 0
33.(i)

The median of an equilateral triangle is increasing at the rate of {tex}2 \sqrt{3}{/tex} cm/s. Find the rate at which its side is increasing.

OR
(ii)

Sum of two numbers is 5. If the sum of the cubes of these numbers is least, then find the sum of the squares of these numbers.
34.

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}sin 2x tan-1(sin x) dx
35.(i)

In answering a question on a multiple choice test, a student either knows the answer or guesses. Let {tex}\frac { 3 } { 5 }{/tex} be the probability that he knows the answer and {tex}\frac { 2 } { 5 }{/tex} be the probability that he guesses.  Assuming that a student who guesses at the answer will be correct with probability {tex}\frac { 1 } { 3 }{/tex}. What is the probability that the student knows the answer, given that he answered it correctly?

OR
(ii)

A box contains 10 tickets, 2 of which carry a prize of ₹ 8 each, 5 of which carry a prize of ₹ 4 each, and remaining 3 carry a prize of ₹ 2 each. If one ticket is drawn at random, find the mean value of the prize.

SECTION - E

This section comprises of 3 case study/passage-based questions of 4 marks each with two sub-parts. First two case study questions have three sub - parts (I), (II), (III) of marks 1, 1, 2 respectively. The third case study question has two sub - parts (I) and (II) of marks 2 each.

Case Study-I
36.

An organization conducted bike race under two different categories - Boys and Girls. There were 28 participants in all. Among all of them, finally three from category 1 and two from category 2 were selected for the final race. Ravi forms two sets B and G with these participants for his college project.
Let B = {b1, b2, b3} and G = {g1, g2}, where B represents the set of Boys selected and G the set of Girls selected for the final race.

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

How many relations are possible from B to G?
(ii)

Among all the possible relations from B to G, how many functions can be formed from B to G?
(iii)

Let R: B {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} B be defined by R = {(x, y): x and y are students of the same sex}. Check if R is an equivalence relation.

OR
(iii)

A function f: B {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} G be defined by f = {(b1, g1), (b2, g2), (b3, g1)}.
Check if f is bijective. Justify your answer.

Case Study-II
37.

Gautam buys 5 pens, 3 bags and 1 instrument box and pays a sum of ₹ 160. From the same shop, Vikram buys 2 pens, 1 bag and 3 instrument boxes and pays a sum of ₹ 190. Also Ankur buys 1 pen, 2 bags and 4 instrument boxes and pays a sum of ₹ 250.

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Convert the given above situation into a matrix equation of the form AX = B.
(ii)

Find |A|.
(iii)

Find A-1.

OR
(iii)

Determine P = A2 - 5A.

Case Study-III
38.

An equation involving derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variables is called a differential equation. A differential equation of the form {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = F(x, y) is said to be homogeneous if F(x, y) is a homogeneous function of degree zero, whereas a function F(x, y) is a homogenous function of degree n if F{tex}(\lambda \text x, \lambda \mathrm{y}){/tex} = {tex}\lambda^{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} F(x, y). To solve a homogeneous differential equation of the type {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = F(x, y) = {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\text x}\right){/tex}, we make the substitution y = vx and then separate the variables.
(i)

Show that (x2 - y2) dx + 2xy dy = 0 is a differential equation of the type {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}= {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex}.
(ii)

Solve the above equation to find its general solution.

An organization conducted bike race under two different categories - Boys and Girls. There were 28 participants in all. A differential equation of the form {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = F(x, y) is said to be homogeneous if F(x, y) is a homogeneous function of degree zero, whereas a function F(x, y) is a homogenous function of degree n if F {tex}(\lambda \text x, \lambda \mathrm{y}){/tex} = {tex}\lambda^{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} F(x, y). General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.

SECTION - A

21.(i)

Find the value of k for which the function f given as
f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{cl} \frac{1-\cos x}{2 x^2}, & \text { if } x \neq 0 \\ \mathrm{k} \;\;\;\;\;\;\;, & \text { if } x=0 \end{array}\right.{/tex} is continuous at x = 0.

Solution:

f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{cl} \frac{1-\cos x}{2 x^2}, & \text { if } x \neq 0 \\ \mathrm{k} \;\;\;\;\;\;\;, & \text { if } x=0 \end{array}\right.{/tex} 
Here, Given that F(x) continues function
{tex}\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow 0^-} f(\text x){/tex} = {tex}\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow 0^+} f(\text x){/tex} = f(0)
f(x) = {tex}\frac{1-\cos \text x}{2 \text x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \sin ^2 \frac{\text x}{2}}{2 \text x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\left(\frac{\sin \frac{\text x}{2}}{2 \frac{\text x}{2}}\right)^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow 0} f(\text x){/tex} = {tex}\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{4}\left(\frac{\sin \frac{x}{2}}{\frac{x}{2}}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
So, if f is continuous at x = 0, then f(0) = {tex}\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow 0} f(x){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}

 2

If x = a cos t and y = b sin t, then find {tex}\frac{\mathrm{\text d}^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}{/tex}.

Solution:

Given x = a cos t and y = b sin t, we have
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex} = -a sin t and {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex} = b cos t
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex}{tex}\cdot{/tex}{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{b \cos t}{-a \sin t}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{b}{a}{/tex} cot t
{tex}\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex}(-{tex}\frac{b}{a}{/tex} cot t){tex}\cdot{/tex}{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\mathrm{b}}{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} cosec2 t{tex}\cdot{/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{-\mathrm{a} \sin \mathrm{t}}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{b}{a^2} \cdot \frac{1}{\sin ^3 t}{/tex} or {tex}-\frac{b}{a^2}{/tex} cosec3 t

= {tex}\tan ^{-1}\left[2 \times \frac{1}{2}\right]+\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}

So, the required vector equation of the line passing through(1, 2, -1) is
{tex}\vec{r}{/tex} = {tex}(\hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}-\hat{k}){/tex} + {tex}\lambda(7 \hat{\imath}-5 \hat{\jmath}+\hat{k}){/tex}
Cartesian equation of the line is
{tex}\frac{\text x-1}{7}=\frac{\text y-2}{-5}=\frac{\text z+1}{1}{/tex}

Required area = {tex}\int\limits_2^4 \frac{1}{2}{/tex}(8 - y) dy
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left|8 y-\frac{y^2}{2}\right|_2^4{/tex}
= 5

25.(i)

If vectors {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} are such that {tex} | \vec { a } | = 3,{/tex} {tex} | \vec { b } | = \frac 2 3{/tex} and {tex} \vec { a } \times \vec { b }{/tex} is a unit vector, then write the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }.{/tex}

Solution:

We have to find the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }.{/tex}Given, {tex} | \vec { a } | = 3{/tex}and {tex} | \vec { b } | = 2 / 3{/tex}
Let {tex} \theta{/tex} be the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }.{/tex}
Also, given {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = 1{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad | \vec { a } | | \vec { b } | \sin \theta = 1 \Rightarrow 3 \times \frac { 2 } { 3 } \sin \theta = 1{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 \sin \theta = 1 \Rightarrow \sin \theta = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \Rightarrow \theta = \frac { \pi } { 6 }{/tex}

 2

OR
(ii)

Find the area of the parallelogram whose adjacent sides are determined by the vectors {tex}\vec a = \hat i - \hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 2\hat i - 7\hat j + \hat k{/tex}

Solution:

Given: Vectors representing two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are
{tex}\vec a = \hat i - \hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 2\hat i - 7\hat j + \hat k{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \vec a \times \vec b = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\hat i}&\hat j&{\hat k} \\ 1&{ - 1}&3 \\ 2&{ - 7}&1 \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = {tex}\hat i\left( { - 1 + 21} \right) - \hat j\left( {1 - 6} \right) + \hat k\left( { - 7 + 2} \right){/tex} = {tex}20\hat i + 5\hat j - 5\hat k{/tex}
Now Area of parallelogram = {tex}\left| {\vec a \times \vec b} \right|{/tex}
= {tex} \sqrt {400 + 25 + 25} = \sqrt {450} = 15\sqrt 2 {/tex} sq. units

26.

Prove that the determinant {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex} is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}.

Solution:

Let {tex}\Delta = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

Expanding along first row,

{tex}\Delta = x\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - x}&1 \\ 1&x \end{array}} \right| - \sin \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&x \end{array}} \right| {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&{ - x} \\ {\cos \theta }&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = x\left( { - {x^2} - 1} \right) - \sin \theta \left( { - x\sin \theta - \cos \theta } \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left( { - \sin \theta + x\cos \theta } \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x{\sin ^2}\theta {/tex} {tex}+ \sin \theta \cos \theta - \sin \theta \cos \theta + x{\cos ^2}\theta{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x\left( {{{\sin }^2}\theta + {{\cos }^2}\theta } \right) {/tex} = -x3 - x + x = - x3 which is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}

If {tex}\vec { a } = \hat { i } + \hat { j } + \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex} then find a unit vector perpendicular to both {tex}\vec { a } + \vec { b }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { a } - \vec { b }{/tex}.

Solution:

According to the question,
{tex}\vec { a } = \hat { i } + \hat { j } + \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex}
Let the required unit vector be
{tex}\vec { r } = x \hat { i } + y \hat { j } + z \hat { k },{/tex}
then, {tex}\sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + y ^ { 2 } + z ^ { 2 } } = 1{/tex}
squaring on both sides, we get 
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x ^ { 2 } + y ^ { 2 } + z ^ { 2 } = 1{/tex} ...(i)
Now, {tex}\vec { a } + \vec { b } = ( \hat { i } + \hat { j} + \hat { k } ) + (\hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ){/tex}
{tex}\vec { a } - \vec { b } = ( \hat { i } + \hat { j } + \hat { k } ) - ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ){/tex}{tex}= - \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex}
Since, {tex}\vec { r }{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec { a } + \vec { b }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { a } - \vec { b },{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad \vec { r } \cdot ( \vec { a } + \vec { b } ) = 0 \text { and }{/tex}{tex} \vec { r } \cdot ( \vec { a } - \vec { b } ) = 0{/tex}
{tex}( x \hat { i } + y \hat { j } + z \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 2 \hat { i } + 3 \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k } ) = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 x + 3 y + 4 z = 0{/tex}, ...(ii)
{tex}( x \hat { i } +y \hat { j } + z \hat { k } ) \cdot ( - \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } ) = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow -y - 2z = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow y = - 2z{/tex} 
On putting the value of y in Eq. (ii), we get
{tex}2x + 3 (-2z) + 4z  =0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = z
On substituting the value of x and y in Eq. (i), we get
{tex}z ^ { 2 } + 4 z ^ { 2 } + z ^ { 2 } = 1 \Rightarrow 6 z ^ { 2 } = 1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad z = \pm \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } }{/tex}
then, {tex}x = \pm \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } }{/tex} and {tex}y = \mp \frac { 2 } { \sqrt { 6 } }{/tex}
Hence, the required vectors are
{tex}\frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } } \hat { i } - \frac { 2 } { \sqrt { 6 } } \hat { j } + \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } } \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\frac { - 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } } \hat { i } + \frac { 2 } { \sqrt { 6 } } \hat { j } - \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } } \hat { k }.{/tex}

Find the distance between the lines:
{tex}\vec{r}{/tex} = {tex}(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} + {tex}\lambda(2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex};
{tex}\vec{r}{/tex} = {tex}(3 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}){/tex} + {tex}\mu(4 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}+12 \hat{k}){/tex}

Solution:

Here
{tex}\overrightarrow{a_1}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k}, {/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}=2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{a_2}=3 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}, {/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{b_2}=4 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}+12 \hat{k}{/tex}
Here, {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}_1}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}_2}{/tex} are parallel vectors.
{tex}\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}=2 \hat{\imath}+\hat{\jmath}-\hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{\imath}+3 \hat{\jmath}+6 \hat{k}{/tex}
Thus, {tex}\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right) \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{\imath} & \hat{\jmath} & \hat{k} \\ 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 2 & 3 & 6 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}9 \hat{i}-14 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex}
Distance between the lines = {tex}\left|\frac{\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right) \times \vec{b}}{|\vec{b}|}\right|{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{81+196+16}}{\sqrt{4+9+36}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt{293}}{7}{/tex} units.

30.(i)

Differentiate sec-1{tex}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\right){/tex} w.r.t. sin-1{tex}\left(2 x \sqrt{1-x^2}\right){/tex}.

Solution:

Let x = sin {tex}\theta{/tex}. Then
U = sec-1{tex}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\sin ^2 \theta}}\right){/tex} = sec-1{tex}\left(\frac{1}{\cos \theta}\right){/tex}
= sec-1 (sec {tex}\theta{/tex}) = {tex}\theta{/tex} = sin-1x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dU}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-\text x^2}}{/tex}
and V = sin-1 {2 sin {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{1-\sin ^2 \theta}{/tex}}
= sin-1 [2 sin {tex}\theta{/tex} cos {tex}\theta{/tex}] = {tex}2\theta{/tex} = 2 sin-1 x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dV}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{2}{\sqrt{1-\text x^2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dU}}{\text {dV}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac {\text {dU}}{\text {dx}}}{\frac {\text {dV}}{\text {dx}}}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
Note: If the substitution is made as x = cos {tex}\theta{/tex}, answer will be {tex}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

(ii)

If y = tan x + sec x, prove that {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)^{2}}{/tex}

Solution:

We have y = tan x + sec x. Differentiating w.r.t. x, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\sec ^{2} x+\sec x \tan x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\cos ^{2} x}+\frac{\sin x}{\cos ^{2} x}=\frac{1+\sin x}{\cos ^{2} x}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{1+\sin x}{(1+\sin x)(1-\sin x)}{/tex}
Thus, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{1-\sin x}{/tex}
Now, differentiating again w.r.t. x, we get
{tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=\frac{-(-\cos x)}{(1-\sin x)^{2}}=\frac{\cos x}{(1-\sin x)^{2}}{/tex}

33.(i)

The median of an equilateral triangle is increasing at the rate of {tex}2 \sqrt{3}{/tex} cm/s. Find the rate at which its side is increasing.

Solution:

In an equilateral triangle, median is same as altitude. Let ‘h’ denote the length of the median (or altitude) and ‘x’ be the side of {tex}\triangle {\text {ABC}}{/tex}.

Then, h = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \text x{/tex} or x = {tex}\frac{2 \text h}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} ...(i)
It is given that {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dh}}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex} = {tex}2\sqrt 3{/tex} So, by (i) we have
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \frac{\text {dh}}{\text {dt}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dt}}{/tex} = 4
Thus, the side of {tex}\triangle {\text {ABC}}{/tex} is increasing at the rate of 4 cm/sec.

Sum of two numbers is 5. If the sum of the cubes of these numbers is least, then find the sum of the squares of these numbers.

Solution:

Let the two numbers be x and y. Then, x + y = 5 or y = 5 - x
Let S denote the sum of the cubes of these numbers. Then
S = x3 + y3 = x3 + (5 - x)3
{tex}\frac{d S}{d x}{/tex} = 3x2 - 3(5 - x)2 = 15(2x - 5)
Now {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dS}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = 0, gives x = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
Showing S is minimum at x = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
So, the two numbers are {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + y2 = {tex}\frac{25}{4}+\frac{25}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{25}{2}{/tex}

34.

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}sin 2x tan-1(sin x) dx

Solution:

Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}sin 2x tan-1(sin x) dx.
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}2 sin x cos x tan-1 (sin x) dx
Put sin x = t so that cos x dx = dt
Thus, I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^1{/tex}t tan-1 t dt
= {tex}2\left[\left|\frac{\text t^2}{2} \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right|_0^1\right.{/tex} - {tex}\left.\int\limits_0^1 \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \cdot \frac{t^2}{2} \mathrm{dt}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}2 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} - {tex}\int\limits_0^1 \frac{\mathrm{t}^2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \mathrm{dt}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\int\limits_0^1\left[1-\frac{1}{1+t^2}\right] \mathrm{dt}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-|\mathrm{t}|_0^1{/tex} + {tex}\left|\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right|_0^1{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-1+\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}-1{/tex}

35.(i)

In answering a question on a multiple choice test, a student either knows the answer or guesses. Let {tex}\frac { 3 } { 5 }{/tex} be the probability that he knows the answer and {tex}\frac { 2 } { 5 }{/tex} be the probability that he guesses.  Assuming that a student who guesses at the answer will be correct with probability {tex}\frac { 1 } { 3 }{/tex}. What is the probability that the student knows the answer, given that he answered it correctly?

Solution:

Let E1: Event that the student knows the answer
E2: Event that the student guesses the answer
E: Event that the answer is correct
Here, E1 and E2 are mutually exclusive and exhaustive events.
{tex}\therefore \quad P \left( E _ { 1 } \right) = \frac { 3 } { 5 } \text { and } P \left( E _ { 2 } \right) = \frac { 2 } { 5 }{/tex}
Now,{tex}P \left( \frac { E } { E _ { 1 } } \right) = P{/tex}(the student answered correctly, given the knows the answer)=1
{tex}P \left( \frac { E } { E _ { 2 } } \right) = P{/tex}(the student answered correctly, given he gusses){tex}= \frac { 1 } { 3 }{/tex}
The probability that the student knows the answer given that he answered it correctly is
given by {tex}P \left( \frac { E _ { 1 } } { E } \right){/tex}
By using Baye's theorem, we get
{tex}P \left( \frac { E _ { 1 } } { E } \right) = \frac { P \left( \frac { E } { E _ { 1 } } \right) \cdot P \left( E _ { 1 } \right) } { P \left( \frac { E } { E _ { 1 } } \right) P \left( E _ { 1 } \right) + P \left( \frac { E } { E _ { 2 } } \right) P \left( E _ { 2 } \right) }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 \times \frac { 3 } { 5 } } { 1 \times \frac { 3 } { 5 } + \frac { 1 } { 3 } \times \frac { 2 } { 5 } } = \frac { \frac { 3 } { 5 } } { \frac { 3 } { 5 } + \frac { 2 } { 15 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { \frac { 3 } { 5 } } { \frac { 15 + 10 } { 15 } } = \frac { 3 \times 3 } { 25 } = \frac { 9 } { 25 }{/tex}

P(X = 8) = {tex}\frac{2}{10}{/tex}, or {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
P(X = 4) = {tex}\frac{5}{10}{/tex}, or {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
P(X = 2) = {tex}\frac{3}{10}{/tex}

X 8 4 2
P(X) {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{3}{10}{/tex}
XP(X) {tex}\frac{8}{5}{/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{6}{10}{/tex}

Hence, Mean value of X = {tex}\Sigma{/tex}X P(X) = {tex}\frac{8}{5}{/tex} + 2 + {tex}\frac{6}{10}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{42}{10}{/tex} or ₹ 4.20

  2. |A| = 5(4 - 6) - 3(8 - 3) + 1(4 - 1) = -22

Case Study-III
38.

An equation involving derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variables is called a differential equation. A differential equation of the form {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = F(x, y) is said to be homogeneous if F(x, y) is a homogeneous function of degree zero, whereas a function F(x, y) is a homogenous function of degree n if F{tex}(\lambda \text x, \lambda \mathrm{y}){/tex} = {tex}\lambda^{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} F(x, y). To solve a homogeneous differential equation of the type {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = F(x, y) = {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\text x}\right){/tex}, we make the substitution y = vx and then separate the variables.
Based on the above, answer the following questions:

  1. Show that (x2 - y2) dx + 2xy dy = 0 is a differential equation of the type {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}= {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex}. (2)
  2. Solve the above equation to find its general solution. (2)
Solution:
  1. (x2 - y2)dx + 2xydy = 0
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y^2-\text x^2}{2 \text {xy}}{/tex}
    = {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)^2-1}{2\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)}{/tex}
    = {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right){/tex}
  2. y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\text v+\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}
    {tex}\text v+\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text v^2-1}{2 \text v}-\text v{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1-\text v^2}{2 \text v}{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{2 \text v}{1+\text v^2} \text {dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |1 + v2| + log |x| = log C
    or {tex}\text x\left(1+\frac{\text y^2}{\text x^2}\right){/tex} = C
    or x2 + y2 = Cx
